column1: # Orbital elements of hypothetical and fictitious celestial bodies
# Used in astrological traditions; these bodies do not exist physically.
# epoch_jd:       Reference epoch as Julian Day (TT)
# mean_anomaly:   Mean anomaly at epoch (degrees; may include linear T term
#                 as "const + rate * T" where T = Julian centuries from epoch)
# eccentricity:   Orbital eccentricity (may include T term)
# arg_perihelion: Argument of perihelion in degrees (may include T term)
# asc_node:       Longitude of ascending node in degrees (may include T term)
# inclination:    Orbital inclination in degrees (may include T term)
# source:         Bibliographic reference for the elements
# "T" is Julian centuries elapsed from epoch_jd:
# Lines starting with '#' are comments. Blank lines are ignored.
# Alfred Witte introduced the first four in 1914-1919; Sieggrün added four more.

column2: as published in various Hamburg School
